@@ -565,6 +565,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-7om4b","title":"PORT: vis + nvis (NetBSD vis(3) single-byte encoders)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) single-byte encoders: vis(dst, c, flags, nextc) writes the visual encoding of byte c into dst (NUL terminated), returning a pointer to the trailing NUL. nvis(dst, dlen, c, flags, nextc) is the bounded variant returning NULL on overflow. The nextc parameter disambiguates \\NNN sequences when octal output could otherwise blur into a following digit; with our 3-digit-padded octal it has no effect, but we accept and document it. Companion to the strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis quartet shipped in bd-i18zl. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: thin shims in stdio_abi.rs over the existing core::stdio::vis::encode_byte primitive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:55:27.265158817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:59:26.051091735Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:59:26.049065399Z","close_reason":"shipped: stdio_abi vis+nvis over core::stdio::vis::encode_byte; 10 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7rxtm","title":"fuzz: add coverage-guided harness for strftime format-string parser","description":"strftime is a format-string parser (~50 specifiers + flags + length modifiers + locale-sensitive output) with no current fuzz coverage. Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_strftime.rs that exercises frankenlibc_core::time::format_strftime with arbitrary format bytes + arbitrary BrokenDownTime fields and a fixed output buffer. Differential check vs libc::strftime is hard (locale state matters); start with crash-detector + invariant checks: no panic, no buffer overrun (return value <= maxsize), determinism on repeated calls. Per /testing-fuzzing skill (Archetype 1).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:06:47.199509348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:17:01.459756827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:17:01.457528289Z","close_reason":"fuzz_strftime committed + two i32 overflow panics found and fixed: iso_week (bd-7rxtm used to pass tm_yday/tm_wday through i32 arithmetic) and %U/%W week-number formatting. All three sites widened to i64. Regression test format_strftime_does_not_overflow_on_extreme_tm_fields pins i32::MIN/MAX/edge inputs. 22K-exec fuzz pass clean after fixes. Committed ae472b5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7smr","title":"bug: closure gate should not treat stale .beads as source","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:42:52.918118286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T14:05:46.500275585Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T14:05:46.500003144Z","close_reason":"Closure gate now defaults to matrix source to avoid stale beads; beads source opt-in via env","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-7t4wz","title":"P0: gcvt writes past tracked small output buffer","description":"[CRITICAL] The gcvt ABI wrapper constructs a mutable slice from the caller's raw buffer using ndigit + 32 bytes, capped at 512, without consulting malloc_abi::known_remaining(buf). A tracked caller allocation smaller than that assumed size can therefore be turned into an overlong &mut [u8] and written past before the core formatter truncates to that artificial slice size.\n\nEvidence:\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs:3218 accepts the raw caller buf pointer.\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs:3226-3228 assumes buf_size = ndigit.max(0) + 32 (min 512 cap) and calls from_raw_parts_mut(buf, buf_size).\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs:3229 passes that slice to frankenlibc_core::stdlib::gcvt, which writes rendered bytes plus a NUL up to the slice length.\n- The module already imports known_remaining and uses it broadly for other stdlib pointer surfaces, but gcvt does not cap the writable slice by the tracked allocation.\n\nRepro shape: p = malloc(1); call gcvt(123.456, 10, p). Expected hardened/TSM behavior is to cap to known_remaining(p) or reject safely. Actual code creates a 42-byte mutable slice from a 1-byte tracked allocation and may write beyond it.\n\nFix direction: compute tracked = known_remaining(buf as usize); choose a writable length bounded by tracked when present, and return buf after writing only within that length. Add an ABI regression using malloc-backed 1-byte and 2-byte buffers so the wrapper preserves the canary/neighbor bytes while still NUL-terminating where possible.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex-gcvt-bounds","created_at":"2026-04-29T05:52:03.010351239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T07:03:30.110428079Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T07:03:30.110428079Z","close_reason":"fixed: gcvt caps malloc-tracked output buffers with known_remaining; validation: stdlib_abi_test gcvt, diff_gcvt_cases, cargo check/clippy -p frankenlibc-abi, UBS","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gcvt","safety","stdlib"]}
 {"id":"bd-7t6b","title":"syscall: mount_setattr invalid dirfd should not override NULL pathname EINVAL","description":"Host glibc still returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(-1, NULL, 0, NULL, 0), so pathname validation wins over the bad dirfd in this degenerate case. Add focused ABI coverage for that precedence rule.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:42.214693289Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:55:03.836123521Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:55:03.833885623Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7thd","title":"syscall: eventfd_write invalid fd should preserve EBADF even for forbidden all-ones payload","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for eventfd_write(-1, UINT64_MAX), so the invalid-fd contract dominates the forbidden payload. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:37:58.438270320Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.456939420Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.454702537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7v4oe","title":"setjmp_abi tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests flakes under high parallelism","description":"## Repro

```bash
for i in {1..8}; do
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test \
    -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- --test-threads=16 \
    | grep -E "FAILED|invalid size|signal:"
done
```

After ~3-4 iterations the unit test
`setjmp_abi::tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests`
asserts FAILED. Running with `--test-threads=1` does not reproduce.
Found while stress-testing the bd-wbqeo per-thread reentry refactor;
the failure is unrelated to that change (also reproduces against
prior commits — file history shows the test pattern is unchanged).

## Suspected cause

The setjmp/longjmp test uses a shared
`OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, JumpRegistryEntry>>>` keyed by env
pointer address (crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/setjmp_abi.rs:44). The
unit test creates a stack-local `marker: [u64; 16]` and uses its
address as the env_addr key. Two test functions running on different
worker threads get distinct stack addresses, but a single worker
thread that runs tests sequentially reuses the same stack frame
addresses across tests — the registry entry from a prior test
remains for that key and is overwritten by the next capture. The
new entry's bytes should match the new capture's mem_bytes, but
under contention or specific scheduling the read-back of mem_bytes
in restore_env compares against a stale or partially-updated entry,
returning EFAULT (22) instead of triggering the deferred
ENOSYS-panic path the test asserts.

The test then panics with errno=22 reason=`invalid_or_foreign_jump_context`
instead of errno=38 reason=`deferred_longjmp_transfer_backend`.

## Fix candidates

1. Test-only: each test should remove its registry entry on exit
   (RAII guard that calls `registry().lock().remove(&env_addr)`
   when the test scope ends).
2. Production: registry could key on (thread_id, env_addr) instead
   of just env_addr — this would handle both intentional cross-
   thread longjmp (illegal per POSIX, EINVAL is correct) and the
   stack-frame-reuse case across sequential tests on the same worker.
3. Test-only: serialize the setjmp tests with a #[serial] attribute
   or a shared TEST_LOCK Mutex so they don't race for stack address
   reuse.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) — test-only flake, doesn't affect production behavior.
But it does mask real failures by polluting CI noise. Suggest fix #1
(simplest) or #3 (most robust) as the swarm has time.","notes":"Fixed in 6ee22896: RegistryEntryGuard RAII wrapper added to all six setjmp_abi unit tests. Validated by 12x stress with --test-threads=16: zero longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests failures. Three OTHER unrelated flakes surfaced in same sweep — filed separately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:26:42.985457536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
